title: erigeron neomexicanus
intro: Erigeron neomexicanus, also known as New Mexican fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Erigeron neomexicanus include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Erigeron neomexicanus can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
